title: US completes third round of strikes in Iran after hitting 140 targets, Qatar intercepts missile | Top Updates
content:
• The US Central Command (CENTCOM) completed its third round of airstrikes in Iran on July 11, hitting approximately 140 Iranian military targets.
• During three nights of strikes this week, US forces hit over 300 targets in Iran to degrade its ability to attack commercial vessels in the strategically important Strait of Hormuz.
• The strikes were in response to the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) attacking the Cyprus-flagged container ship M/V GFS Galaxy in the Strait of Hormuz, prompting Iran to shut the strait 'until further notice'.

title: Vinod Khosla net worth — All about new owner of Seattle Seahawks who sealed $9.6 billion record deal
content:
• Vinod Khosla's company agreed to a record deal to purchase the Seattle Seahawks for $9.6 billion.
• Khosla Ventures became the first venture firm to invest in OpenAI after closing a $122 billion funding round, valuing OpenAI at $852 billion as of March 2026.
• In 1982, Vinod Khosla cofounded computer hardware firm Sun Microsystems with Andy Bechtolsheim, Bill Joy and Scott McNealy.

title: Ashwini Vaishnaw shares timeline for India's first bullet train, Mumbai-Ahmedabad section to be launched in 2027
content:
• Union Minister for Railways Ashwini Vaishnaw announced that the first section of the Mumbai-Ahmedabad bullet train, between Surat and Bilimore, will be launched "next year," with the entire Mumbai-Ahmedabad section targeted for 2027.
• Hyderabad is set to become a "high-speed hub" with three bullet train corridors allocated by Prime Minister Narendra Modi: Pune-Hyderabad, Hyderabad-Chennai, and Hyderabad-Bengaluru.
• Prime Minister Narendra Modi has allocated a significant railway budget of ₹5,400 crore to Telangana, aiming to bring revolutionary changes to the state.

title: Teen accused in Apalachee High School mass shooting to change plea, says report: What we know about the case?
content:
• Colt Gray, accused in the September 2024 Apalachee High School mass shooting in Georgia, is scheduled to appear in court on July 24 for a change-of-plea hearing.
• Colt Gray's father, Colin Gray, was convicted in March of second-degree murder, marking him as the first adult charged in connection with a school shooting in Georgia and the third such parent nationally.
• Colt Gray faces a total of 55 counts, including malice murder, and could face up to 30 years for second-degree murder charges, with a total potential sentence reaching up to 180 years.

title: New York Times journalists ordered to testify over Qatari-gifted Air Force One security report
content:
• The US Department of Justice (DOJ) subpoenaed four New York Times journalists, Julian E. Barnes, Eric Lipton, Tyler Pager, and Eric Schmitt, after they reported on alleged security concerns involving the new Qatari-gifted Air Force One.
• The Trump administration spent approximately $400 million upgrading the Qatari-gifted Air Force One before it was placed into service.
• FBI Director Kash Patel and senior Justice Department officials met at the White House on Friday to discuss the investigation before the subpoenas were issued to the journalists.

title: Europe weighs Oman-backed navigation fee plan in Hormuz to resolve dispute with Iran: Report
content:
• European governments are examining an Oman-backed proposal for voluntary navigation service fees for ships using the Strait of Hormuz, which requires backing from the UN's maritime agency and must not be mandatory.
• Britain's Deputy Prime Minister David Lammy warned that mandatory charges for passage through the Strait of Hormuz would be 'disastrous,' a view shared by Oman, which controls most of the navigable waters in the strait.
• At an International Maritime Organization (IMO) Council meeting in London, a resolution by Gulf and European countries condemning Iran over attacks on commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz failed after Russia and China declined to support it.

title: 2 lakh micro food processing enterprises covered under credit-linked subsidy scheme 'PMFME': Govt
content:
• The Pradhan Mantri Formalisation of Micro Food Processing Enterprises (PMFME) scheme, launched in 2020 and extended till September 2024, has provided around Rs 6,000 crore in total subsidy and leveraged over Rs 20,300 crore in investments for over 2 lakh micro enterprises.
• Under the PMFME scheme, individual micro food processing enterprises receive a 35% subsidy, with a maximum limit of Rs 10 lakh, for setting up or upgrading their units.
• The PMFME scheme offers a seed capital of Rs 40,000 per Self Help Group (SHG) member and a 35% credit-linked subsidy (maximum Rs 3 crore) for Farmer Producer Organisations (FPO), Farmer Producer Companies (FPC), and SHGs to develop common infrastructure.

title: VOC Port signs MoU with Port of Antwerp-Bruges for green & digital port development
content:
• V.O. Chidambaranar Port Authority (VOC Port) signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Port of Antwerp-Bruges International NV and APEC Antwerp-Flanders Port Training Center, both from Belgium.
• The three-year MoU aims to explore cooperation in capacity building, green port initiatives, and digital transformation of port operations.
• The MoU was signed by Rajesh Soundararajan, Deputy Chairperson of V.O. Chidambaranar Port Authority, and Kristof Waterschoot, Managing Director of Port of Antwerp-Bruges International NV and APEC Antwerp-Flanders Port Training Center VZW.

title: Monsoon deficit narrows sharply, but weak Kharif sowing remains a key risk: Report
content:
• India's cumulative rainfall deficit narrowed to 15 per cent below normal as of Wednesday, a sharp improvement from 38 per cent a week earlier.
• During the week ended July 8, India received 83.6 mm of rainfall, which was 45 per cent above the normal level of 57.5 mm.
• As of Sunday, total Kharif sowing was 20.8 per cent lower year-on-year, with oilseeds down 39.3 per cent, while sugarcane was the only major crop to record a marginal increase of 1.6 per cent.

title: India seeks review of proposed tariff, cites flaws in USTR's report on forced labour probe
content:
• The USTR proposed a 12.5 per cent tariff on imports from 54 economies, including India and China, and a 10 per cent tariff on imports from Canada, Ecuador, the European Union, Indonesia, Mexico, and Pakistan, after issuing its findings on June 3 in a forced labour investigation.
• India, represented by Joint Secretary in Department of Commerce Brij Mohan Mishra, urged the USTR to reconsider its proposed 12.5 per cent tariff, citing inconsistencies in its Section 301 investigation into forced labour concerns.
• The USTR launched two separate Section 301 investigations on March 11 and 12, 2026, covering 60 economies over concerns related to forced labour and excess industrial capacity.
